Chief Complaint
Neck and back pain following MVC.
HPI
Patient presents via private vehicle with neck and low back pain following a rear-end MVA earlier today. Impact was
significant. Patient denies prior neck pain or back issues. No LOC reported.

• Eyes: Normal.
• MSK: Neck pain.

Patient presents for evaluation of chronic neck and back pain following a motor vehicle accident.

Patient reports difficulty with ADLs including dressing and grooming. Sitting tolerance limited to 20 minutes.

flags.label: Conservative Care Only (No Surgery or Injection)
flags.detail: No surgical procedure or injection documented. Conservative care only.
flags.defense_argument: Conservative-only treatment (no injections, no surgery) indicates the injury was not severe enough to warrant escalated intervention.
flags.plaintiff_counter: Conservative management is clinically appropriate for many documented soft tissue and disc injuries and reflects responsible clinical judgment rather than mild injury severity. Many patients with significant disc pathology are managed conservatively per established guidelines.

flags.label: Short Treatment Duration (<30 days)
flags.defense_argument: A 13-day treatment course is consistent with minor, self-resolving injury rather than a significant traumatic condition.
flags.plaintiff_counter: Treatment duration may be limited by insurance authorization, financial constraints, or documented discharge at maximum therapeutic benefit — not by symptom resolution. Ongoing pain and functional limitations are documented regardless of formal treatment end.

flags.label: Low Physical Therapy Visit Count (<6)
flags.defense_argument: Only 4 PT encounter(s) documented, suggesting limited functional impairment inconsistent with a serious injury claim.
flags.plaintiff_counter: Low visit count may reflect early discharge at functional goals, insurance authorization limits, or transfer to home exercise program — not absence of injury. Even brief PT courses document functional deficits at intake.

flags.label: No Objective Neurological Deficit Documented
flags.detail: No EMG positive result and no objective neurological deficit documented.
flags.defense_argument: Absence of documented neurological deficit (no positive EMG, no documented sensory or motor deficit) indicates the injury is subjective complaint only.
flags.plaintiff_counter: Neurological deficits are not required for compensable injury. Documented disc pathology, pain, and functional limitation are independently compensable. EMG is not ordered in every case and its absence does not indicate absence of radicular pathology.

case_severity_index.band: Moderate soft tissue with objective support
case_severity_index.profile: Profile: Disc pathology documented; ED + imaging + PT course documented; <14 day treatment course.

risk_factors: Conservative Care Only (No Surgery or Injection) [MED]
settlement_model_report.posture_text: This case has compensable injuries but notable risk factors that need to be addressed. Focus on obtaining additional supporting documentation and addressing causation gaps before demand. Case Severity Index: 5.5/10.

negotiation_strategy.opening_strategy: Anchor near specials. Emphasize continuity of care and documented mechanism.
counter_positioning: Use consistency of presenting complaints as credibility anchor
Keep focus on documented objective evidence
